properties.default: ={{ (() => { const hosts = {"ap-southeast-1":"https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com","us-east-1":"https://dashscope-us.aliyuncs.com","cn-beijing":"https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com","cn-hongkong":"https://cn-hongkong.dashscope.aliyuncs.com"}; const region = $self.region; if (region === "eu-central-1") { return "https://" + $self.workspaceId + ".eu-central-1.maas.aliyuncs.com"; } return hosts[region] || hosts["ap-southeast-1"]; })() }}

properties.name: clientSessionKeepAlive
properties.description: Whether to keep alive the client session. By default, client connections typically time out approximately 3-4 hours after the most recent query was executed. If the parameter clientSessionKeepAlive is set to true, the client’s connection to the server will be kept alive indefinitely, even if no queries are executed.

properties.description: Whether credentials should be included in the query. Occasionally, some servers may not parse the Authorization header correctly (if you see a “Consumer key is missing” error when authenticating over SSL, you have a server issue). In this case, you may provide the consumer key/secret as query string parameters instead.
